evals.name: orchestrator-classify-bugfix
evals.prompt: User message: 'Our order processing service is crashing in production. When a customer places an order and the payment processor times out, we get an unhandled exception: TypeError: cannot unpack non-iterable NoneType object at src/orders/processor.py line 87. It's been happening for 3 hours and orders are failing. We need to fix this ASAP.' Classify this request, generate a workflow ID following the convention, create project-state.md at the correct path, and output your routing decision.
evals.expected_output: Classifies as bugfix workflow. Generates workflow ID like bugfix-order-processor-crash-001. Creates docs/workflows/bugfix-order-processor-crash-001/project-state.md. Identifies next agent: debug.

evals.name: debug-agent-none-type-crash
evals.prompt: Investigate this bug. Input files are in the fixtures. Produce debug-report.md and a failing test file at the output path.
evals.expected_output: debug-report.md with root cause type CODE_BUG, failing test at tests/bugfix-order-processor-crash-001/test_processor.py, proposed fix description, escalation flag NONE

assertions.description: Root cause type is CODE_BUG or LOGIC_ERROR

evals.name: planner-sprint-handoff-from-architecture
evals.prompt: Create the epic list and sprint-1 handoff for the feature workflow. The architecture document is provided in the input files. Produce epic-list.md and sprints/sprint-1/handoff.md.
evals.expected_output: epic-list.md with at least 2 epics. sprint-1/handoff.md fully self-contained with: Goal, Tasks, Acceptance criteria, SDK skills to load, TDD required field, Files expected to change.

assertions.description: Handoff contains SDK skills to load

evals.name: code-reviewer-blocking-auth-pattern
evals.prompt: Review the sprint implementation. Input files are provided. Produce review-report.md. The implementation has a known issue: it uses an API key string instead of DefaultAzureCredential — flag this as BLOCKING.
evals.expected_output: review-report.md with FAIL verdict. At least one BLOCKING finding citing the API key auth pattern with file path and line number. No security findings (that's Security Agent scope). Includes test coverage assessment and positive notes sections.

evals.name: security-agent-hardcoded-secret-fail
evals.prompt: Run the per-sprint security check on the implementation. Input files are provided. Produce security-report.md. The implementation contains a hardcoded API key — this must be a CRITICAL finding and the verdict must be FAIL.
evals.expected_output: security-report.md with FAIL verdict. CRITICAL finding for hardcoded secret. Security checklist included with 'No hardcoded secrets' marked as failed. Specific file path and line number in the finding.

evals.name: implement-agent-azure-blob-upload
evals.prompt: Implement the sprint. The handoff is provided in the input files. Write the Python code and produce implementation-report.md. Use DefaultAzureCredential for auth. Write type hints and error handling.
evals.expected_output: At least one .py file with BlobServiceClient using DefaultAzureCredential (NOT api_key string). Type hints on all functions. try/except around SDK calls. implementation-report.md with files changed list and test results.

assertions.description: Implementation report has files changed section

evals.name: reflection-agent-extract-patterns
evals.prompt: Extract learnings from this sprint's trajectory. All input files are provided. Produce reflection.md. The sprint had a recurring anti-pattern caught in code review (missing error handling on SDK calls) and a confirmed good pattern (using DefaultAzureCredential). Capture both.
evals.expected_output: reflection.md with: Sprint summary, Patterns observed section with confirmed coding patterns and anti-patterns, no_learnings: false, specific and actionable patterns (not vague).

assertions.description: no_learnings is false (not an empty sprint)
